result.summary: GitHub exposes REST and GraphQL APIs plus the `gh` CLI; auth is via PAT/OAuth/GitHub App tokens for APIs, MCP support is documented but endpoint auth couldn't be pinned.

github_pat.label: GitHub personal access token
github_pat.setup: Go to [Developer settings](https://github.com/settings/tokens) and create a personal access token. Prefer a fine-grained PAT for least privilege; classic PATs also work for some APIs. Use the token as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` (or `token <token>` for older tooling) when the docs/API allow it. Choose the minimum repo/org/user scopes or fine-grained permissions needed for your call.

github_oauth_token.label: GitHub OAuth app user access token
github_oauth_token.setup: Create an OAuth app in [Developer settings](https://github.com/settings/developers), then complete the GitHub OAuth flow to mint a user access token for your app. Use the resulting OAuth access token wherever GitHub accepts OAuth tokens.

github_app_installation_token.label: GitHub App installation access token
github_app_installation_token.setup: Register a GitHub App in [Developer settings](https://github.com/settings/apps). Create the app's private key, then exchange a short-lived JWT for an installation access token for the target installation. Use that installation token to call GitHub APIs on behalf of the installed app.
